Paul, an apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Christ Jesus (the Messiah) by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
To the church of God which is at Corinth, and to all the saints (God's people) throughout Achaia (southern Greece):

To the church of God in Corinth, together with all the saints throughout Achaia:

47_1_12.ESV: For our boast is this, the testimony of our conscience, that we behaved in the world with simplicity  and godly sincerity, not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God, and supremely so toward you.
47_1_12.NASB: For our proud confidence is this: the testimony of our conscience, that in holiness and godly sincerity, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God, we have conducted ourselves in the world, and especially toward you.
47_1_12.NLT: We can say with confidence and a clear conscience that we have lived with a God-given holiness and sincerity in all our dealings. We have depended on God’s grace, not on our own human wisdom. That is how we have conducted ourselves before the world, and especially toward you.
47_1_12.WEB: For our boasting is this: the testimony of our conscience, that in holiness and sincerity of God, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God we behaved ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.
47_1_12.CSB17: Indeed, this is our boast: The testimony <sup>ⓛ</sup> of our conscience <sup>ⓜ</sup> is that we have conducted ourselves in the world, and especially toward you, with godly sincerity and purity, not by human wisdom <sup>ⓝ</sup> but by God’s grace. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
47_1_12.AMP: This is our [reason for] proud confidence: our conscience testifies that we have conducted ourselves in the world [in general], and especially toward you, with pure motives and godly sincerity, not in human wisdom, but in the grace of God [that is, His gracious lovingkindness that leads people to Christ and spiritual maturity].
47_1_12.MSG: Now that the worst is over, we're pleased we can report that we've come out of this with conscience and faith intact, and can face the world—and even more importantly, face you with our heads held high. But it wasn't by any fancy footwork on our part. It was God who kept us focused on him, uncompromised.
47_1_12.NIV:
Paul’s Change of Plans
Now this is our boast: Our conscience testifies that we have conducted ourselves in the world, and especially in our relations with you, in the holiness and sincerity that are from God. We have done so not according to worldly wisdom but according to God’s grace.

47_5_5.NRSVCE: He who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who has given us the Spirit as a guarantee.

47_5_6.NKJV: So we are always confident, knowing that while we are at home in the body we are absent from the Lord.

47_5_11.NKJV: Knowing, therefore, the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are well known to God, and I also trust are well known in your consciences.

47_5_11.MSG: That keeps us vigilant, you can be sure. It's no light thing to know that we'll all one day stand in that place of Judgment. The Ministry of Reconciliation

47_13_11.AMP: Finally, believers, rejoice! Be made complete [be what you should be], be comforted, be like-minded, live in peace [enjoy the spiritual well-being experienced by believers who walk closely with God]; and the God of love and peace [the source of lovingkindness] will be with you.
47_13_11.MSG: And that's about it, friends. Be cheerful. Keep things in good repair. Keep your spirits up. Think in harmony. Be agreeable. Do all that, and the God of love and peace will be with you for sure.
47_13_11.NIV:
Final Greetings
Finally, brothers, good-by. Aim for perfection, listen to my appeal, be of one mind, live in peace. And the God of love and peace will be with you.
47_13_11.NRSVCE: Finally, brothers and sisters, farewell. Put things in order, listen to my appeal, agree with one another, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.
